name: Farro-Stuffed Tomatoes (Weir)
recipeIngredient: 1 medium ripe red tomato, seeded and diced
salt and freshly ground black pepper
recipeInstructions:
1. Place the farro in a large bowl and cover with plenty of cold water. Let sit for 4 hours.
2. Drain the farro, then transfer to a saucepan. Cover with plenty of water. Bring to a boil over high heat. Reduce the heat to low and simmer until the grains are tender but still crunchy, 15 to 20 minutes. Drain and let cool.
3. Meanwhile, place the cucumber on paper towels, sprinkle with salt and let rest for 20 minutes. Place in a colander and rinse with cold water for a moment. Dry on paper towels.
4. Combine the cucumber, diced tomato and onion in a bowl. Tear the basil into 1/2-inch pieces and add to the vegetables.
5. Whisk together the vinegar and olive oil. Carefully toss the vinaigrette with the farro and vegetables. Season with salt and pepper. Set aside for 1 hour.
6. Cut a 3/4-inch slice off the stem end of each tomato; save the tops. Using a spoon, carefully scoop out the pulp; chop it and add to the farro and vegetables. Sprinkle the inside of the tomatoes with salt, then turn them upside down on paper towels to drain for 1 hour.
7. Sprinkle the inside of the tomatoes with pepper. Distribute the farro salad evenly among the tomatoes and cover with the reserved tops. Serve at room temperature.
